findings.message: Missing mandatory descriptive label(s): maintainer, version, description. Add LABEL instructions for better image documentation (e.g., LABEL maintainer="email@example.com" version="1.0" description="App description").

Pulling an image based on its digest is security-sensitive. Using tags instead of digests can lead to non-reproducible builds and security vulnerabilities. Tags are mutable and can point to different image versions over time. Use immutable digests (@sha256:...) to ensure you're always using the exact intended image version. Example: FROM ubuntu@sha256:abc123... instead of FROM ubuntu:22.04

Use digest to pin versions of base images. Image tags are mutable and can be updated to point to different images, making builds non-reproducible. Pin the image version using its SHA256 digest (e.g., 'FROM ubuntu:20.04@sha256:abc123...') to ensure you always get the exact same image and guarantee reproducible builds.

WORKDIR instruction should only be used with absolute path. Using relative paths like 'relative/path', './app', or '../parent' can lead to confusion and unexpected behavior. Use absolute paths starting with '/' for clarity and reliability, such as '/app' or '/var/www/html'.
